caption: Vanessa just called — Blake's camp put a hold on 'Rio Nights.' Might actually be the one. Trying not to count chickens but Abuelita is already picking out what to wear 😂
ANDREW. That's your cut, I've been saying it since you played it for me at midnight in your kitchen. Don't you dare downplay this.
Derek's already texting me about tracking the b-side 💀 Nashville will not let a man sit with a feeling for five consecutive minutes
Two seconds of joy first!! Call Rosa right now she will LOSE it
Voice memo number 412 in the library. half of these never become anything and then one of them turns into a hold from Blake's camp. you never know which trash idea is the cut. keep them all 🎙️
Behind the glass at Copperline. Terrell ran the board, Derek paced the room, I sang the same line nineteen times until it stopped sounding like words. that's how you know it's done 🎚️
Sunday tamales the way Abuelita taught me. masa, lard, patience, and a phone propped up so San Antonio can yell at me that I'm spreading it too thin 😂 cafe de olla on the stove
Pair number 14. 1970s Western, found them at an estate sale in Franklin, broke them in walking to the studio. a man can never have too many boots, this is settled law 👢
Balcony garden update: the basil survived, the rosemary is thriving, the cayenne has opinions. East Nashville winter is mild enough that I get to keep my herbs going 🌿
New verse, old wound. wrote about my dad for the first time tonight. nearly twenty years and the song let me say it finally. swipe for the lyric draft, timestamped like always ✍️
Round at the Riverstone Listening Room tonight. four writers, four chairs, one mic. this is the version of Nashville I moved here for 🎸
Backup vocal day. you don't see me in the video, you hear me holding the third above the lead. session singing is the quiet craft that pays my rent and I love it 🎤
Pozole rojo for a Tuesday because the song wasn't coming and the kitchen always knows what to do when the page doesn't. Selena on the JBL, hominy on the simmer 🌶️
34. spent the morning at Sunrise Flow, the afternoon at the kitchen table with a half written song, Mamá on speaker the whole time. grateful for a life that sounds like this 🎂
Dia de los Muertos ofrenda for Papá and Abuelita's people. marigolds, his picture, the pan dulce, a little of the cafe he liked. we don't forget our dead, we set them a place 🕯️
Mix notes with Derek and Terrell. three of five tracked, the b-side might be the best thing I've ever put to tape. EP wraps this winter and then we go pitch it 🎧
Cowrite with a new writer Vanessa set me up with. she hears harmony the way I do, which is rare. left with two thirds of a song and a hook I'm still humming on the Uber home 🎶
Churros and cafe de olla, piloncillo melting down slow. some Sundays the only thing on the schedule is making something with my hands the way my grandmother did ☕
New Harvest choir Sunday. forty voices and a room with no auto tune. you chase a hook all week and then this reminds you why you ever opened your mouth to sing 🙌
Carly Thornton's record went 42 on country radio with a song I helped write. heard it at a San Antonio gas station over Christmas and just stood there. that's the dream, the long slow version of it 🎵
Late night at Copperline. Track three is finally breathing. 🎙️
Cafe de olla and a blank page. Best part of the morning. ☕
New pair of 70s ropers found their way home. Boot count: 15. 👢
Harmonies for days. Grateful for a room full of people who listen.
Abuelita's cast iron, my kitchen, Sunday pozole. Some things don't change.
Bluebird round tonight. Two new ones I've never played live. Wish me luck.
Balcony garden update: the cayenne is winning. 🌶️
Voice memo #417. Somewhere in here is a song. 🎶
Silverbell writers room. Verse two put up a fight and lost.
San Antonio on my mind. Building the ofrenda this weekend. 🕯️
Nashville hot chicken, medium, mac on the side. I have opinions.
Terrell's mix made me tear up in the booth. That's the job.
Sunday choir at New Harvest. The descant carries the whole room.
Demo EP: three down, two to go. Slowest race I've ever run.
Mezcal kind of night. Feeling San Antonio in Nashville. 🥃
Wrote something honest today. Scared to play it, which means it's real.
Radnor Lake before the heat. Songwriting walks hit different.
Vintage Western wear haul with my designer friend. Stage looks incoming.
Pan dulce from Mamá arrived. Homesick and grateful in equal measure.
The hold came through on 'Rio Nights.' Trying not to count chickens. 🐔
